title: Styling Entities and Occurrences in HTML
breadcrumbs: Entity and Occurrence Properties in Dynamic Server Pages
Styling Entities and Occurrences in HTML
text: Styling Entities and Occurrences in HTML When Uniface generates HTML for dynamic server pages (DSPs), it sets HTML class attributes that can be used to style the server page. Empty Occurrences and Entities If no data is present in a component, Uniface generates a default empty occurrence for each entity in a DSP and flags it as empty. Uniface class attributes are applied to the HTML elements representing the empty entity and occurrence. This enables you to define a CSS to suppress or otherwise format the empty elements. Table 1. Empty Element and Occurrence Classes Uniface Class Name Applicable To Styling Example -uocc-empty- Empty occurrences .-uocc-empty- { visibility: hidden; } -uent-empty- Entity with only one occurrence, which is empty .-uent-empty- { visibility: hidden; } An occurrence (and its parent entity) is flagged as empty in the following circumstances: It is the default occurrence. It is the last occurrence and it has been removed or discarded in ProcScript (by remocc or discard ), or in JavaScript (by OccurrenceObject.remove() or OccurrenceObject.discard() ) The entity has been cleared in ProcScript by clear or in JavaScript by EntityObject.clear() . The status of an empty occurrence changes in the following circumstances: The end-user enters data in a field and leaves the field. A value is assigned to a field in ProcScript or JavaScript (using FieldObject.setValue() ) A new occurrence is added or created in ProcScript (by creocc ) or in JavaScript (by EntityObject.createOccurrence() , OccurrenceObject.insertNewAfter() , or OccurrenceObject.insertNewBefore() Note: When the status of an inner entity changes from empty to non-empty, this change is also applied to its parent outer entity (or entities). Related Links Entity and Occurrence Properties in Dynamic Server Pages In Dynamic Server Page components, you can manipulate the widget properties of entities and occurrences in the runtime component using ProcScript or JavaScript. It is not possible to set these properties declaratively. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
